create_analytics_description: Generate the embed snippet for this project's landing page
name: Name
name_help: Human-readable label for this analytics config.
disable_confirm_description: "{{name}}" will stop collecting events until re-enabled.
secret_token_help: Server-generated credential used in the script URL. Shown only once — cannot be changed after creation.
allowed_origins_help: Restrict event collection to specific landing-page hosts (e.g. https://example.com or *.example.com). Leave empty to accept events from any site.
allowed_origins_empty_warning: No restrictions — events will be accepted from any origin.
allowed_origins_snippet_warning_body: This analytics config is restricted to {{count}} allowed origin(s). Events from any other origin will be rejected.
snippet_description: Paste this <script> tag into your landing page. The loader is INERT by default — it writes nothing and POSTs nothing until you call window.analytics.activate() from your consent UI (GDPR / ePrivacy).
snippet_help: Self-contained loader (~2 KB, no dependencies). It writes only to its own localStorage (analytics.visitorId) and sessionStorage (analytics.sessionId) keys when activated. No cookies, no third-party storage.
snippet_consent_help: Consent gate (call from your cookie banner):
snippet_consent_note: activate() and deactivate() are idempotent. Call deactivate() on withdrawal so the next activate() starts a fresh session with a new visitorId.
stats_pageviews_help: Auto-fired pageview events (page loads, SPA navigations).
stats_events_help: Events fired via window.analytics.event('name', {...}). Form submissions live in the Forms module and are NOT counted here.
stats_unique_sessions_help: Counted by sessionId (stored in sessionStorage; new per browser tab/visit).
stats_no_data: No events yet — paste the snippet into your landing page and open it in a browser.
stats_devices_help: Bucketed by screen width: mobile <768, tablet 768..1023, desktop >=1024.
stats_hourly_heatmap_help: When your visitors are active. Brighter = more pageviews + events.
stats_realtime_help: Pageviews and events received in the last 30 minutes.
config_section_analytics_empty: No analytics yet. Enable analytics to start collecting pageviews.
confirm_delete_description: Are you sure you want to delete "{{name}}"? All collected events will also be deleted. This cannot be undone.
event_tracking_help: Custom events (no-op until activated): window.analytics.event('signup', { plan: 'pro' }).
loader_self_contained: Self-contained loader, no cookies, no external dependencies.
data_collected_intro: Every event the loader sends (after activation) carries the fields below. Use this list verbatim in your cookie policy / privacy notice so the data subject is informed at the point of consent (GDPR Art. 13).
data_field_path: path — current URL path + query string (no scheme, no host). Example: /pricing?utm=email
data_field_referrer: referrer — the URL of the previous page (document.referrer). Empty if the user typed the URL or came from HTTPS→HTTP.
data_field_title: title — document.title of the current page (pageview events only).
data_field_locale: locale — navigator.language, truncated to 10 chars. Example: hu-HU, en-US.
data_field_screen: screenWidth / screenHeight — window.screen dimensions in pixels. Used to bucket visitors into mobile / tablet / desktop.
data_field_visitor_id: visitorId — random UUID, persisted in localStorage.analytics.visitorId. Resets on deactivate(); survives across sessions until cleared.
data_field_session_id: sessionId — random UUID, persisted in sessionStorage.analytics.sessionId. Resets on deactivate(); new per browser tab.
data_field_timestamp: ts — epoch milliseconds when the event fired (Date.now()).
data_collected_server_title: Auto-captured by the server from the HTTP request:
data_field_ip: IP address — req.ip, stored as INET. Used only for rate limiting and aggregate unique-visitor counting; not exposed back to the public.
data_field_user_agent: User-Agent — request header, truncated to 500 chars. Used for browser/device breakdowns.
data_collected_not_collected: The loader does NOT collect: cookies of any kind, email/name/phone, geolocation, fingerprinting signals (canvas, fonts, audio context), cross-site identifiers, or any data from third-party domains.
data_collected_storage: Storage: the only persistent IDs are localStorage.analytics.visitorId and sessionStorage.analytics.sessionId (first-party only, not shared with any third party). All other fields are sent over HTTPS to the BE and persisted in the analytics_events table on the operator's own server.
